Qualifications

Demonstrated ability to understand the requirements to work safely in an industrial environment. Actual work experience in this type of environment would be an asset;
Demonstrated ability with Microsoft Office programs, SharePoint and Teams;
Demonstrated ability to communicate proficiently in English in order to comprehend verbal safety instructions, reading of technical manuals, communications with staff and writing procedures;
Class 5 Drivers licence required, with experience driving on gravel roads considered an asset.

About This Opportunity
This opportunity requires the successful candidate to travel to the remote work site, following a 2 week in and 2 week out work schedule. The assignment will expose the successful applicant to a wide variety of the equipment used in hydro generation, transmission and distribution and the operating and maintenance requirements for the generating station. The applicant will also be exposed to the data requirements for a modern Maintenance Management System.

CMH is an award-winning [Recipient of a 2019 Clean Energy BC (CEBC) award for Operational Excellence] run-of-the-river hydroelectric project - comprising the Forrest Kerr, McLymont and Volcano Hydroelectric Facilities. We are located in Northwestern BC, with the Coast Mountain Range to the west and Skeena Mountains to the east, approximately 100 km from the town of Stewart and within the Golden Triangle in Tahltan Territory.

CMH is owned by Axium, Manulife and the Tahltan Nation. With two active mines in proximity, and an estimated $365.9M of exploration expenditures incurred in the Northwest throughout 2021, CMH - which generates 300 megawatts, enough to power 225,000 homes - is providing a source of clean power to local Tahltan communities and to the exploration and mining industry.

Additional Information
A permanent camp has been constructed for the operations team. The facilities include private rooms with bathrooms, a weight room, entertainment area and other amenities.
The schedule will be 14 days on followed by 14 days off. Note that travel days occur on days outside of the 14 day shift.
Travel costs from the student's primary residence (in British Columbia or Alberta) airport to Terrace B.C. are covered by the company. Travel from Terrace to the worksite is done by driving with other co-workers in company vehicles.

To apply for this position, please email your resume to humanresources@cmhydro.com.

We thank all applicants for their interest in our organization, but only those candidates selected for interviews will be contacted.

IT Systems Co-op (6931)

Requisition ID 6931
Location New Afton Mine - Kamloops, British Columbia

Make an impact at Canada's premier mining company.

When you advance your career at New Gold, you are joining a Canadian-focused intermediate gold mining company engaged in the operation, development and exploration of mineral properties. Our mission is be a leading intermediate gold producer, driving responsible and profitable mining in a way that creates sustainable value for our employee, shareholders, the communities in which we live and work and our environment. To learn more about New Gold, visit our website at www.newgold.com. To learn more about our culture and people practices, visit the Career page.

New Gold attracts and hires highly skilled individuals. By joining our team, you can look forward to
A stimulating values-based work environment
A culture of collaboration and inclusion
Inspiring colleagues and approachable leaders
Career development opportunities
A deeply rooted commitment to responsible mining, health and safety and community engagement
A competitive total compensation program
A comprehensive benefits package including a retirement savings plan

New Afton Mine
Situated 10 kilometers west of Kamloops, British Columbia, the New Afton Mine is an underground gold and copper producing site.
Kamloops is a city of 90,000 people, located 350 kilometers east of Vancouver. The area provides the opportunity to live and work in a vibrant community with its own university and within easy reach of world class ski resorts, championship golf courses, mountain biking trails, popular recreational lakes and award-winning vineyards.

About the Role
Schedule 5x2, Monday- Friday
Position Reports To IT Supervisor

Position Overview
We currently have an exciting opportunity for a student to join our New Afton team for an 8 or 12 month work term commencing in September 2024. This position will provide support to the IT Department.

Key Responsibilities
Reporting to the IT Supervisor, the IT Systems Co-op Student works as a team member in the effective installation, configuration, operation, maintenance and troubleshooting of systems and communications hardware, software and related infrastructure.
The successful applicant will aid in ensuring reliable, stable operation of on-site systems and communications using leading edge technologies & software.
This individual will participate in technical research and development to enable continuing innovation within the infrastructure.
The successful applicant will gain experience with enterprise grade systems including HyperV, Cisco unified communications, Windows Server, Microsoft O365, Sharepoint. Microsoft Azure, Exchange 2016/Exchange Online, ERP solutions, site security, CCTV video, collaboration/messaging solutions amongst other things.

Strong communication skills and customer service orientation is required.

Compensation

New Gold is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We are pleased to consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, protected veterans' status, Indigenous status or any other legally protected factors. Ideal Start Date August 19, 2024

Positions Available 1

Ever wanted to get hands-on experience with innovative talent technology tools used by a global company? This is your chance! As a Co-op student on our Global Talent Technology (GTT) team based in Vancouver, you'll be at the forefront of supporting multiple geographically dispersed client groups. Working alongside an agile team, you'll gain valuable experience in operational support, configuration, deployment, automation and more, impacting the entire employee lifecycle.

Our ideal candidate has a high level of attention to detail and can use their data analysis skills to identify areas for process optimization. Your technical background will be instrumental in understanding and supporting our various talent technology tools.

Responsibilities
Be a courageous safety leader, adhere to and sponsor safety and environmental rules and procedures
Triaging and resolving incoming support tickets and requests regarding application errors, and resolving critical issues across the supported application stack in a timely manner
Conducting regular data cleansing, data modelling, and data analysis with Python, Excel and Power BI
Commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) product evaluation, configuration, integration, testing, deployment, and support
Finding innovative ways to automate repetitive and manual tasks using scripts in addition to the development of lean self-serve web applications using Power Automate
Preparing data for monthly and quarterly dashboards, and crafting Power BI dashboards

Proficiency in Python with knowledge and understanding of data packages like pandas and JSON
Demonstrable understanding of how to use RESTful APIs, including different authorization frameworks (e.g. OAuth 2.0, SAML 2.0, SCIM)

Ability to clearly articulate sophisticated technological concepts to non technical collaborators
Basic understanding of Agile methodologies and their benefits
Driven and efficient with a passion for learning new technologies and able to work well both independently and with others
Experience with Power Platform applications such as Power Apps, Power BI, and Power Automate is a plus

In addition to excellent compensation, Teck Co-ops receive a variety of advantages including but not limited to
Financial assistance to help with travel costs and Co-op fees
Opportunity to develop technical skills and gain practical experience while applying academic knowledge to real-world mining operations and projects
Enjoy mentorship and guidance from experienced specialists in their respective fields
Receive primary consideration for professional positions upon graduation

$3,900 - $4,900 a month
Salary is commensurate with the number of academic semesters and prior Co-op experiences completed.

Teck Resources Ltd. is Canada's largest diversified resource company committed to responsible mining and mineral development with business units focused on steelmaking coal, copper, zinc and energy. Teck hires more than 200 co-op students each year to work at our various Canadian operations. We have received several awards and recognitions that are worth noting In 2023, Teck was named as one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People, which recognizes companies for exceptional programs and performance in attracting and retaining younger workers. Teck was also selected as one of Canada's Top 100 Employers from 2018 to 2023. Teck has been recognized as one of the 2023 Global 100 Most Sustainable Corporations by Corporate Knights, marking the fifth straight year Teck has been named to the list.

Ideas at Work
Digital technologies are transforming our industry and contributing to improvements in every step of the mining process. We enhance our productivity, safety, sustainability, and growth by putting smarter ideas to work and creating advancements in our technology and our practice. At Teck, we don't just embrace ideas, we employ them. We are fostering a culture where employees are encouraged to bring ideas forward, test innovative approaches and implement technologies that can help make us a better company.

Professional benefits of working as a co-op with Teck Resources Ltd
Our values guide every decision we make as a company and as individual employees. In everything we do, Teck is led by our values of safety, sustainability, integrity, respect, excellence, and courage. A co-op with Teck will offer you the opportunity to work on challenging projects alongside a team of professionals who are experts in their respective fields, you will experience a combination of projects in the field as well as in the office. Teck offers a competitive salary for our co-ops as well as assistance with travel costs and co-op fees. Make an impression! Successful co-ops with Teck are given first consideration for professional positions upon graduation.

5wqvsg.Job Description:
Arctic Wolf, with its unicorn valuation, is the leader in security operations in an exciting and fast-growing industry-cybersecurity. We have won countless awards for our excellence in security operations and remain dedicated to providing an industry-leading customer and employee experience.

Our mission is simple End Cyber Risk. We are looking for Co-ops in our Research & Development and Product Management teams to be part of making that happen.

Join us and help revolutionize the way that network security is delivered to small and medium businesses. Our hardworking team is passionate about building a cloud-based solution that will solve the real world security problems that IT professionals face.

You'll use various languages/technologies such as Golang, RabbitMQ, JavaScript, PostgresSQL, AWS Cloud Services, GitHub, Python, and Jenkins to implement features.

Job Responsibilities (depending on role)
End to end ownership of the design, development and deployment of new features in innovative cyber security products
Follow software engineering best practices such as unit/component testing, code reviews, continuous integration using tools such as Git and Jenkins
Build and maintain high-level knowledge of cyber security threats
Identify new and innovative ways to handle cyber security incidents more effectively and efficiently
Debug and troubleshoot problems in test and production environments
Collaborate effectively with the user experience designer, product manager and developers to bring powerful designs to life
Deliver solutions at the intersection of Big Data, Cloud, and Web

About Arctic Wolf
At Arctic Wolf we're cultivating a collaborative and productive work environment that welcomes a diversity of backgrounds, cultures, and ideas to make our teams even stronger as we grow globally. We've been named one of the 50 Most Innovative Companies in the world for 2022 (Fast Company)-and the 2nd Most Innovative Security Company. This is in addition to consecutive awards from Top Workplace USA (2021, 2022), Best Places to Work - USA (2021, 2022) and Great Place to Work - Canada (2021, 2022).

Our Values
Arctic Wolf recognizes that success comes from delighting our customers, so we work together to ensure that happens every day. We believe in diversity and inclusion, and truly value the unique qualities and unique perspectives all employees bring to the organization. And we appreciate that-by protecting people's and organizations' sensitive data and seeking to end cyber risk- we get to work in an industry that is fundamental to the greater good.

We celebrate unique perspectives by creating a platform for all voices to be heard through our Pack Unity program. We encourage all employees to join or create a new alliance. See more about our Pack Unity here.

We also believe and practice corporate responsibility, and have recently joined the Pledge 1% Movement, ensuring that we continue to give back to our community. We know that through our mission to End Cyber Risk we will continue to engage and give back to our communities.

Arctic Wolf is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics, or any other basis forbidden under federal, provincial, or local law. Arctic Wolf is committed to fostering a welcoming, accessible, respectful, and inclusive environment ensuring equal access and participation for people with disabilities. As such, we strive to make our entire?employee?experience as accessible as possible and provide?accommodations?as required for candidates and employees with disabilities and/or other specific needs where possible. Please let us know if you require any accommodations by emailing recruiting@arcticwolf.com.

Security Requirements
Conducts duties and responsibilities in accordance with AWN's Information Security policies, standards, processes and controls to protect the confidentiality, integrity and availability of AWN business information (in accordance with our employee handbook and corporate policies).
Background checks are required for this position.

mmx2v.Job Description:
ViVitro Labs is seeking an analytical, motivated and solutions focused Coop Student with demonstrated product development experience to join our growing team in Victoria, BC. We are an internationally recognized and growing company that provides specialized test equipment solutions to support medical device innovations in the cardiovascular space.

In our small, innovative team you will enjoy the challenge of working on a variety of R&D projects where you will conceptualize, prototype, design, and document products with a user centric approach in mind. Reporting to the Director of Product Development and working closely with the Director of Technology and Innovation, you will apply your knowledge of engineering design principles and bring practical solutions to ensure our products are of high quality and reliability to maintain ViVitro's brand.

Do you possess a willingness to learn and the ability to manage concurrent tasks? Are you inquisitive and analytical in nature? Do you bring excellent communication skills and the ability to "think outside the box"? If you answered "yes" you will thrive in this role!

We are actively looking for the right student to join our team of engineers in Victoria, BC, Canada for the Fall term (September 2024 - December 2024).

Salary
Hourly rate for 3rd year student is $23.56 and hourly rate for 4th year student is $24.64

Key Responsibilities
Work as an integral member of the engineering team to design and build innovative products
Create software system concepts
Build proof of concept prototypes
Test concept prototypes
De-bug issues and identify the root cause
Design subsystems/systems according to design control procedures
Document design according to design/document control procedures
Verify design and validate product prototypes
Transfer design to manufacturing
Provide other technical assistance as needed
Report on project progress as requested
Attend training sessions as needed to expand your technical knowledge and skills
mmx2v.Qualifications:
Education and Experience Required/Desired
Working towards a Bachelor/Master degree in Software Engineering or related fields is required
Previous COOP experience is desired
Previous design project experience is desired
Availability for a 4-month (or 8-month) work term is desired
Senior students are desired

Knowledge/Skills/Competencies Desired
Willingness to be challenged intellectually
Capability to learn new things
Troubleshooting abilities
Experience with C# programming
Experience with Git/Github or Tuleap
Experience with Labview
Experience with algorithm design
Knowledge of engineering design processes
Experience with electronic components and firmware development
Familiarity with working in a regulated product development environment
Knowledge of Video Image Processing with OpenCV or equivalent

About ViVitro Labs ViVitro Labs provides contract testing services and test equipment solutions to support medical device innovations in the cardiovascular space. An internationally recognized and trusted source for testing, with two ISO 17025 accredited laboratories and a reputable history of more than 25 years, ViVitro Labs is a small company relied upon by hundreds of organizations in over 40 countries for its expertise in the testing industry's most advanced cardiovascular devices.

ViVitro Labs works closely with universities, medical device manufacturers and regulatory agencies to support their research, regulatory, and production requirements. As a leader in the heart valve testing industry, ViVitro is an active participant in several ISO working groups. ViVitro Labs is a small, tight-knit team looking at significant international growth in the next 5 years.

Don't meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and people of color are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. At ViVitro Labs, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyways. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles!

1dnthf.Job Description:
About WISI America

At WISI, we have spent almost 100 years focused on implementing the most innovative and reliable technology solutions. We provide video delivery solutions to video operators around the world. Founded in 1926, WISI is one of the world's pioneers in broadband reception and distribution technology. In 2015, WISI acquired Inca Networks, now operating as WISI America. WISI America's R&D facility produces industry-leading video transcoding and monitoring platforms.

The Role

We're looking for a local, talented and motivated Software QA Test Engineers (Co-op), who are in their final year of studies, that can help us reach our goal of achieving 100% CI/CD test & deployment automation of our carrier-grade video transcoder product that executes in the data centers of cable/video providers. Our ideal candidate will be in their final year of school with an interest in eventually pursuing a career as a software developer. You'll work closely with a team of talented software engineers, and you'll gain experience testing embedded firmware with hard-real-time execution requirements, as well as front-end UI applications. You'll apply black-box, white-box and chaos engineering methods, using test automation & scripting technologies like Jenkins, Python, and Selenium.

The company is located in Pitt Meadows, BC a suburb of Metro Vancouver. We are in a gorgeous location near walking and biking trails, away from the hustle and bustle of the city and we are proud to have been certified as a Great Place To Work after a thorough and independent analysis conducted by the Great Place To Work Institute Canada for the 5th consecutive year. We are also on the 2023 list of Best Workplaces in Canada.
1dnthf.Qualifications:
You are
Data driven. You believe in using measurements to assess and improve product quality and robustness.
Have solid computer science fundamentals. You'll be able to read and understand the code that you'll be testing, including asynchronous operations and multi-threaded code.
Meticulous about documentation. You'll naturally document your own code sufficiently well so that your successor will be able to understand, maintain and extend it.
Driven to be better. You love learning industry best practices and how to apply them to your own work.

Responsibilities
Develop test automation infrastructure & tools. Write test cases and scripts to automate deployment and test.
Monitor and report product quality improvements or regressions through numeric measurement.
Investigate test failures and provide defect reports to developers for resolution.
Work directly with developers and QA Lead to invent and implement new ways to test product robustness.

Requirements
Must be a 4th year (final year) student, ideally with an interest in pursuing a career in software development (as a software developer eventually)
Undergraduate Major in computer science or engineering
Experience with Python, Java, Bash scripting is required
C/C++ and Selenium would be an asset
Experience with real-time embedded programming would be an asset
Experience with CI/CD tools would be an asset (Jenkins)
Excellent verbal and written English
Must live in the Greater Vancouver area and can come into the office 3 days per week (a vehicle will be necessary). The last bus stop is about a 20-minute walk to the company office so this would be best suited to someone who has access to a vehicle. Free parking is provided.

What's in it for you
A chance to work on cutting edge technology in a quality-demanding industry.
Monthly salary of $3,800 (unless completing a Masters course then this is increased) + 2 weeks' vacation (pro-rated for the 8-month term) and all the in-office perks - we have many.
Learning is one of our core values so there are many opportunities for development.
Flexible-Hybrid work environment
Birthday Day off - after all this is YOUR day.
Paid Volunteer Hours.
Be a part of a team of incredibly talented, driven, fun and supportive teammates.
Contribute to WISI's award-winning Culture. We're a Certified Great Place To Work and on the 2021 list of Best Workplace in Canada.

Must be eligible to work in Canada

We are an equal opportunity employer and a very diverse company! We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender identity, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, age, language or disability status.

9b992q.Job Description:
Summary / Role Purpose

You will be part of the Ansys Cloud, Solutions & Enablement Business Unit to develop?the?next generation simulation platform for the democratization and re-use of Ansys technologies. We are building a culture of automation-first for delivering Ansys SaaS products, with many different teams building and deploying applications into a SaaS environment. Each team needs to have a consistent pattern and example for how to integrate their development quality process(es) into our continuous integration, continuous deployment tooling. These patterns need an automated way to start at a quality baseline, and progressively improve that baseline. You will have the opportunity to work with senior QA Automation and Web Application developers to create a tool which improves both overall code quality and the developer experience.

This is a xx hour per week paid position starting August 2024 and concluding December 2024.

Key Duties and Responsibilities
Employ automation tools for Quality Assurance processes as part of the application development lifecycle
Work with open source tools to create continuous integration utilities as a re-usable pattern
Work with senior colleagues to define and implement quality metrics and monitors, as inputs for automating code changes and deployments
Analyze and link operations outcomes to development practices
Develop a tool to increase developer output quality, while improving the developer experience
Gain exposure to cloud hosted web applications across both the development and service lifecycles
9b992q.Qualifications:

Pursuing a bachelors degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering or related technical degree with significant programming experience.
Exposure to web application, and/or 'full-stack' software development
Knowledge of general cloud-application architectures and patterns
Working with a Source control system (git, svn) and any Integrated Development Environment
A keen interest in advancing and improving the overall developer experience, and your own application developer skillset

Preferred Skills and Experience

Any experience from below is desirable and preferred. Being open to new tools and methods is a must!
Experience in *nix Shell (ash/bash/zsh) scripting
Experience in Node.js/Python scripting or development
Experience with a Continuous Integration Continuous Deployment tool (CircleCI, Github Actions, Jenkins, Travis)
Experience with any unit or integration testing framework
Experience with any cloud-based computing
Experience with any observability or monitoring tools (Datadog, New Relic, OpenTelemetry, Splunk)

If you have ever created a bot to achieve a task or respond to inputs, even from just following a tutorial, this might be for you.

Do you have a passion for the environment and clean tech, and a drive to change our world for the better? Are you eager to build your career in a growing company, enjoying getting exposure to a variety of tasks, and enjoy new challenges? Keep reading! We may have your dream Co-op!

At HTEC, we're unlocking the potential of hydrogen to reduce climate change and air pollution. Based in Vancouver, we design, build, and operate hydrogen fuel supply solutions to support the deployment of hydrogen fuel cell electric vehicles. Every day, we're delivering safe, reliable, convenient, sustainable, and low-cost hydrogen to customers and consumers, how, when, and where they need it.

We run a values-based business so fit comes first. Sure, we've got a list of knowledge, skills, and abilities we're looking for in the successful candidate, and we lay those out below. But making sure our values align is critical. Read our values and if we are a match and you can deliver on the job responsibilities and requirements, we hope to hear from you!

We are looking for a Tier 1 HelpDesk and Workstation Support Co-op student to join our team for a 4-month term. This position is multi-faceted and would involve providing support for large existing projects, as well as managing small-scale projects. Guidance and support would be provided from a team of experienced and helpful engineering colleagues.

Responsibilities
HTEC strives to provide all Co-ops with great on the job experience, so the selected candidate could be assigned a wide array of tasks, including
Tier 1 Basic Helpdesk Support including troubleshooting of hardware and software problems
Computer equipment setup, installation and maintenance
Routine Microsoft 365 & SharePoint Administration
Implementing customized applications and task automation using Microsoft Power Platform
Creation and compiling of IT documentation and policies

IT related helpdesk experience
Comprehensive knowledge of Microsoft Windows and Office 365 applications
Microsoft 365, SharePoint and Azure administration experience
Microsoft Power Platform implementation experience
Relevant industry certifications (A+, Security+, CCNA, etc.)
Strong documentation skills
HTEC offers a creative, cohesive work environment and continuous opportunity for development. Candidates are requested to apply with their resume by July 19, 2024 for a 4-month fall placement with an option to renew to longer if mutually agreed upon. HTEC appreciates interest from all applicants, however only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

About the job

Looking to launch your career at the cutting edge of healthcare? Join Sanofi for a chance to develop with mentoring and guidance from inspirational leaders while helping to make an impact on the lives of countless people worldwide. As a Co-Op Student Digital Strategy Intern within our Digital Strategy & Operations team, you'll collaborate across Digital and other departments at Sanofi to establish connections and integrate digital and AI technologies on a large scale throughout the entirety of Sanofi's value chain.

We are an innovative global healthcare company with one purpose to chase the miracles of science to improve people's lives. We're also a company where you can flourish and grow your career, with countless opportunities to explore, make connections with people, and stretch the limits of what you thought was possible. Ready to get started?

Main responsibilities
Understanding business data requirements and translate them into technical needs
Partnering with Product Owners, Data Engineers and Data Scientists to design novel solutions
Developing critical strategic documents, using creative mechanisms to collaborate and create consensus
Outline the scope of the work and identify and map out schedules, milestones, and required resources to meet the project objectives
Supporting creating of data product marketing/storytelling materials to take to broader user group and organization
Completing a variety of analyses to identify root causes of potential problems, along with the development of remediation plans.
Visualizing data to tell a story with actionable insights
Designing solutions to business problems in a modular manner
Why choose us?
Bring the miracles of science to life alongside a supportive, future-focused team.
Discover endless opportunities to grow your talent and drive your career, whether it's through a promotion or lateral move, at home or internationally.
Enjoy a thoughtful, well-crafted rewards package that recognizes your contribution and amplifies your impact.

Position Pulsed Power Engineering Co-Op
Location Richmond, BC Canada

General Fusion is transforming how the world is energized by developing the first commercially-viable fusion power plant. Fusion will deliver clean, safe, and on-demand power at industrial scale, providing a powerful complement to renewables and a pathway to a zero-emission grid.

Combining new technologies, proven industrial processes, and advances in fundamental fusion science, General Fusion aims to be the first in the world to develop commercial fusion power. Key performance requirements have been demonstrated at the system level, setting the stage for building a pre-commercial fusion power prototype.

Start September 2024
Term 4, 8, 12 or 16 month placements available.

Key Responsibilities
In this position you will participate in the development of the pulsed power supply for the plasma injector for General Fusion's plasma testing. You will gain experience with high-voltage pulsed-power systems and plasma physics. You will also gain experience with shop tools, heavy lifting equipment, electrical and plasma diagnostic equipment. It's all part of building a world-changing fusion reactor. Typical tasks will include electrical and mechanical design and assembly, fabricating and installing new fixtures and equipment, testing components, and analyzing experimental and test data. No prior experience in plasma physics is necessary, but broad mechanical and electrical aptitude is required.

Applicants with a range of practical vs theoretical and mechanical vs electrical aptitude are encouraged.

y8qllk.Job Description:
Experimental Plasma Physics Data Analysis Co-op Posting

General Fusion is transforming how the world is energized by developing the first commercially-viable fusion power plant. Fusion will deliver clean, safe and on-demand power at industrial scale, providing a powerful complement to renewables and a pathway to a zero-emission grid.

Combining new technologies, proven industrial processes, and advances in fundamental fusion science, General Fusion aims to be the first in the world to develop commercial fusion power. Key performance requirements have been demonstrated at the system level, setting the stage for building a pre-commercial fusion power prototype.

Job Title Experimental Plasma Physics Co-op
Location Richmond BC, Canada
Start September 2024
Term 4, 8, 12 or 16 month placements available.

Key Responsibilities
In this position you will participate in the development of a magnetized plasma experiment in support of General Fusion's magnetized target fusion concept. You will gain experience analyzing the dynamics of high temperature magnetized plasmas and help guide optimization of plasma injector performance by uncovering empirical trends in experimental data. You will gain experience with the measurement of plasma properties using data from magnetic probes, visible light spectroscopy, infrared interferometry, laser scattering, and x-ray diagnostics.

Typical tasks will include using existing code and software tools to examine and find trends in a large set of existing plasma data as well as writing new code to analyze experimental data in new ways. No prior experience in plasma physics is necessary, but broad mathematical problem-solving skills, basic physics understanding, and computer programming experience are required.

Required qualifications include an aptitude for mathematical problem solving, ability to implement numerical algorithms and handle data, understanding of how to generate clear scientific plots, as well as the ability to work both independently and part of team. A basic understanding of electromagnetism will be very helpful.

*Experience with Linux, Python, git, Matlab, and SQL are all additional non-required assets.

v36kc4.Job Description:
Overview

The Actuarial Services department of WorkSafeBC is looking for an Actuarial Co-op student to join our team to help us strive towards our vision.

The co-op student will assist the Actuarial Services department in a variety of functions including setting of assessment rates, liability calculations, actuarial gain and loss analysis of financial results, research studies and responding to requests for data analysis and interpretation from other departments. This is a 12-month temporary opportunity starting in September 2024 with a possibility to extend to 16 months in total.

How you'll make a difference You'll apply what you've learned in your post-secondary program to benefit workers and employers across B.C.

Where you'll work

At WorkSafeBC, we offer a hybrid work model that combines the convenience of working remotely with the dynamism of working in one of our offices, based on the operational needs of the position.

In this role, you'll work primarily from your B.C. home and at least once a week in our Richmond office. Please note training will be on-site.

What you'll do
Utilize database query tools (SQL and MS Access) to extract data from large corporate databases
Analyze data, summarize results, and present and discuss findings for further analysis
Document findings and solutions
Communicate with stakeholders to clarify business requirements
Utilize your technical skills to produce answers to business questions
Perform other ad hoc tasks as required
v36kc4.Qualifications:
Is this a good fit for you?

We're looking for someone who can
Communicate effectively, both in writing and verbally, with a variety of people
Problem-solve solutions and enjoys analyzing business and actuarial questions to propose and implement solutions
Demonstrate tact and discretion when handling confidential data
Effectively collaborate and interact with a range of stakeholders
Demonstrate teamwork and organization skills, and is detail-oriented
Be curious, receptive to learning and willing to ask questions

Your experience and educational background
Currently enrolled in an undergraduate degree program in Actuarial Sciences, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related area.
Enrolled in 3rd year or higher of your academic program.
Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel.
Familiar with database query tools such as SQL or MS Access would be an asset.
Previous work experience and completion of some actuarial exams would be an asset.
Knowledge of Python and/or machine learning would be an asset.

Learn more Hear from past co-op students on what it's like to work here.

Important to know

Before we can finalize any offer of employment, you must
Confirm you're legally entitled to work in Canada
Confirm you're a registered student at a post-secondary institution and is participating in a work integrated learning program
Consent to a criminal record check and successfully meet the criminal record clearance

WorkSafeBC's COVID-19 Employee Mandatory Vaccine Policy (the "Policy") is suspended effective January 9, 2023, however we reserve the right to re-implement it in response to changes in the public health landscape, including public health orders. We are committed to the protection, health, and safety of our employees and our Communicable Disease Prevention Program and related protocols remain in effect.

Who are we?

At WorkSafeBC, we promote safe and healthy workplaces across British Columbia. We partner with workers and employers to save lives and prevent injury, disease, and disability. When work-related injuries and diseases occur, we provide compensation and support injured workers in their recovery, rehabilitation, and safe return to work. We're honoured to serve the 2.49 million workers and 263,000 registered employers in our province.

What's it like to work at WorkSafeBC?

It's challenging, stimulating, and rewarding. Our positions offer diversity and opportunities for professional growth. Every day, the work we do impacts people and changes lives. What we do is important, and so are the people we do it for.

Our ability to make a difference relies on building a team with a rich variety of skills, knowledge, backgrounds, abilities, and experiences that reflects the diversity of the people we serve. We are committed to fostering a welcoming, inclusive, and supportive work culture where everyone can contribute as their best, authentic self.

Our Benefits

As a member of our team, you'll have access to services and benefits that help you get the most out of work - and life. Along with a competitive salary, your total compensation package includes
4% in lieu of benefits
4% in lieu of vacation

Salary $23.05/hour

Learn more Discover who we are.

5pj5dk.Job Description:
Discover TRIUMF, Canada's particle accelerator centre, and one of the world's leading laboratories for particle and nuclear physics and accelerator-based science. TRIUMF's diverse community of nearly 600 multidisciplinary researchers, engineers, technicians, tradespeople, staff, and students create a unique incubator for Canadian excellence, as well as a portal to premier global collaborations. Fueling innovation and improving lives, we are committed to accelerating discovery and shaping a better world.

Equity, diversity, and inclusion are integral to excellence and enhance our ability to create knowledge and opportunity for all. Together, we are committed to building an inclusive culture that encourages, supports, and celebrates the voices of our employees, students, partners, and the people and communities we serve.

TRIUMF's student program typically hires ~40 students each term from across all disciplines who hang together socially and professionally during the term, and beyond. Our program offers young minds a chance to gather, learn, make new friends, and have fun doing so during their time at TRIUMF. We hold weekly ProD sessions for students which include different activities such as conflict resolution, emotional intelligence, critical thinking, and active listening. Additionally, we hold weekly seminars about the different experiments at TRIUMF, and these talks specifically cater to students. Located on the UBC campus makes it easier for students to make friends, create memories, and explore Vancouver all while working at TRIUMF.

TRIUMF's student program is more than just a place to work, its learning, memories, friendships, exploration, and development all clubbed into one.

Come join us to see how we are working to unlock the mysteries of the universe and shape the future. Apply now and be part of our extraordinary journey.

Learn more about the amazing research and work we do at TRIUMF.
https//www.discoverourlab.triumf.ca
http//www.rarestdrug.com

Overview
The PHORWARD (PHotons and nOble liquids for Research in Weak processes and Applied R&D) group is interested in recruiting a student to work on characterizing silicon photomultipliers (SiPMs) for the nEXO experiment. nEXO aims to search for neutrinoless double beta decay, which, if discovered, will alter our understanding of the standard model. This experiment will require extremely accurate photon counting using SiPMs, which represent the most sensitive photodetection technology available. TRIUMF is responsible for the selection and testing of the photodetection element and the selected candidate will play a key role in this.

Responsibilities/Duties
Responsibilities will include the following operation and maintenance of a cryogenic vacuum system, measurement of SiPM characteristics such as photon detection efficiency and reflectivity under vacuum UV light, C++, Python and ROOT programming for experiment automation, data analysis, and modelling semiconductor device characteristics, some statistical modelling.

Learning outcomes
Knowledge of vacuum and cryogenic hardware implementation, programming skills (C++, ROOT, and possibly Python). Development of a theoretical understanding of avalanching semiconductor photodetectors and the physical processes that govern them, in addition to some neutrino and astro-particle physics.

TRIUMF, Canada's national laboratory for particle and nuclear physics, is situated on the vibrant south campus of the University of British Columbia, nestled within the beautiful Pacific Spirit Park in Vancouver, BC.


At TRIUMF, we value our employees and are committed to providing a competitive total rewards package. In addition to a competitive salary, we offer comprehensive benefits that promote the well-being and security of our staff. Our organization provides an excellent opportunity to grow your career in a high-profile national research facility, where you can make a difference.

If you are passionate about science, engineering and technology, driven to excel, and eager to be part of a collaborative and forward-thinking organization, we encourage you to apply. Take the next step in your career journey with TRIUMF.

The position is open to candidates studying Physics, Engineering, or related fields. A specific interest in semiconductor devices and in astro-particle physics, as well as the practical hardware and software implementation of physics experiments, is desirable. Some experience with C++/ROOT is required. Experience with electronics is desirable. A demonstrable motivation to work independently is required.

NUCLEAR ENERGY WORKER
The incumbent will be required to successfully complete the TRIUMF in-house radiation safety training course and be designated as a TRIUMF Nuclear Energy Worker.

Skills required
Analog Electronics, Applied Electronics, C++ Programming Language, CERN ROOT, Data Analysis, Electronics Instrumentation, Neutrino Physics, Particle Physics, Photodetectors, Photonics, Python (Programming Language), Semiconductor Physics, Semiconductors, Technical Writing

Are you interested in clean energy projects and funding landscape for these projects? Not afraid of digging through hundreds of records? Want to sharpen your data management skills of the workplace or for graduate research? Want experience with a cutting edge web-based client relationship management (CRM)?

If you have answered yes to all these questions, apply to join the Community Clean Energy Branch.

Title Community Energy Data Assistant (Co-op Student)

Classification CO-OP LEVEL 1 OR 2

Context
The Community Clean Energy Branch of the BC Ministry of Energy, Mines and Low Carbon Innovation promotes the implementation of clean energy systems in communities across BC, including renewable electricity generation (e.g., solar, wind, biomass), energy efficiency (e.g. deep retrofits), and heating systems (e.g., heat pumps). The Branch operates a Community Energy and Emissions Database (CEED) which is a secure web-based database stored on Microsoft Canada's Dynamics365 Azure cloud with data delivered to users via Microsoft's Dynamics 365 client relationship management (CRM) web application. The branch needs help collecting, collating, cleaning, and analyzing data to support the work to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions in Indigenous and civic communities. The relevant data includes community energy and emissions information, funding applications for clean energy projects, funding program information, clean energy projects, and community outreach information related to communities across BC, including Indigenous Nations and local governments.

Job Overview
The Community Energy Data Assistant will assist Community Clean Energy Branch staff with data remediation (strengthening) efforts and CEED development, gaining a deeper understanding of Microsoft Dynamics/CRM systems; alternative energy systems; clean energy project funding; and BC communities' effort towards climate action, including Indigenous Nations.

Key responsibilities
Conducting technical queries and creating data visualizations for branch staff.
Supporting data clean-up efforts and using data applications to compile, organize, and perform quality assessment on data.
Supporting ongoing database development through providing input into technical requirements, conducting user acceptance testing, and supporting work led by database development consultants.
Working with stakeholders to confirm and update data in the database.

CEED Link https//www2.gov.bc.ca/gov/content/industry/electricity-alternative-energy/community-energy-solutions/community-energy-and-emissions-database-ceed

We believe great businesses thrive on diversity, inclusion, and the contributions of all employees. To that end, we recruit candidates from different backgrounds and foster a work environment that encourages employees to collaborate and learn from each other, completely free of barriers.

We encourage you to apply if your qualifications and experience are a good fit for any of our openings.

Your Role
As User Experience (UX) Research Co-op you will be working with our research team to learn about the needs, wants and behaviour of customers. You will work closely with a researcher and help them conduct qualitative and quantitative research to understand our customers' problems. As part of the research team, you will work with the business to understand their needs and collaborate with designers and developers to inform products based on data-driven insights.

This role provides an excellent opportunity to help solve tough problems and create great experiences that have a significant impact on our customers. You will be working with a team that values critical thinking, creativity and professional growth. If you are curious about people and enjoy using data to understand behavior, this is a great opportunity to grow your skills.

Your Job
Participate in research activities such as meetings, project planning, materials development, etc.
Support a primary researcher in conducting studies from planning to analysis and reporting
Take responsibility for running a research project to address a product development need.
You will develop skills in
Data management including gathering, cleaning and organizing
Data analysis and statistics
Identifying and refining research questions
Selecting appropriate research methods
Developing research materials such as research plans, discussion guides, surveys, etc.

To Apply
Think you're a great fit for this job? Apply on our Global Relay Careers page with your cover letter, resume, and university transcripts in one PDF document. No emails or phone calls please; candidates must be legally authorized to work in Canada.

Compensation
Global Relay advertises the pay range for this role in accordance with provincial law. Salaries are displayed in the form of annual base salary but are paid out semi-monthly for the duration of employment.

Base salary range
$36,000-$56,000 CAD

What you can expect
At Global Relay, there's no ceiling to what you can achieve. It's the land of opportunity for the energetic, the intelligent, the driven. You'll receive the mentoring, coaching, and support you need to reach your career goals. You'll be part of a culture that breeds creativity and rewards perseverance and hard work. And you'll be working alongside smart, talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, with complementary knowledge and skills.
Global Relay is an equal-opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion.

We seek to ensure reasonable adjustments, accommodations, and personal time are tailored to meet the unique needs of every individual.

We understand flexible work arrangements are important, and we encourage that in our work culture. Whether it's flexibility around work hours, workstyle, or lifestyle, we want to ensure our employees have a healthy work/life balance. We support and value a hybrid work model that blends collaboration with the team in the office and focus time from the comfort of your home.
To learn more about our business, culture, and community involvement, visit our website.

shtlbo.Job Description:
About UVic CfAR

The University of Victoria's Centre for Aerospace Research (UVic CfAR) has become a Canadian leader in CubeSat as well as Unmanned Air Systems research, design, integration, and testing. Our team of engineers, post-docs, and graduate and co-op students focus on creating cutting-edge designs from initial concepts to flight operations.

Description

Working in the on-campus CfAR lab, the co-op student will primarily work on developing software systems for CfAR's current satellite project, the follow up to the ORCASat Mission (https//www.orcasat.ca/) . The co-op will work on this 3U CubeSat project currently under development with a delivery to the Canada Space Agency in 2025. Due to the nature of the position, 8 months available is required, starting September 2024.

Responsibilities

Responsibilities will vary based on candidates experience and interests, but may include
Software development for the mission control software which is used for command and control of the spacecraft from the ground station at UVic
Development of embedded software for microcontrollers in the command and data handling subsystem.
Preparation of test scripts and test firmware to assist verification and validation testing of various spacecraft subsystem and the ground station

Overview
Tantalus is a smart grid technology company that transforms aging one-way grids into future-proofed multi-directional grids that improve the efficiency, reliability and sustainability of utilities and the communities they serve. Our solutions are purpose-built to allow utilities to restore power quickly after major disruptions, adapt to rapidly shifting consumer expectations and population shifts, innovate new solutions based on the adoption of distributed energy resources and evolve their grid infrastructure at their own pace without needless cost or complexity. All this gives our user community the flexibility they need to get the most value from existing infrastructure investments while planning for future requirements. Learn more at Tantalus.

Background
Have you ever wanted to be a part of a world-renowned science institution or work in an innovative forward-thinking research and development team? Here's your opportunity to develop your skills and showcase your talents at the Pacific Forestry Centre, Natural Resources Canada (Government of Canada).

The Pacific Forestry Centre is an exciting and fun place to work with full amenities (on-site gym, cafeteria, showers, and bike racks). We are close to major transportation corridors, the Galloping Goose Trail and walking distance to shopping, theaters, a recreation centre, a library, restaurants and easy access to Colquitz Park.

Canada's National Forest Information System (NFIS) provides information about Canada's forests and on sustainable forest management. Canada's National Forest Inventory (NFI) is a statistical sampling of Canada's forests to assess and monitor sustainable development of Canada's forests. As a member of the NFIS and NFI project offices you will be working in a software development team focussing on leading-edge technologies, interoperability issues and spatial warehousing (digital maps and remote sensing/imagery), visualization, analysis, reporting and modelling. Our projects include the use of Java, JavaScript, PHP, XML, AJAX, relational and non-relational databases and distributed secured architectures/infrastructures, cybersecurity, cloud computing, large-big data and digital mapping.

To learn more, visit our website.

Legal Services Branch Overview
The Legal Services Branch (LSB) is the official legal advisor to the government of British Columbia and is charged with ensuring the administration of public affairs is carried out in accordance with the law. LSB assists in fulfilling the Attorney General's role as set out under the Attorney General Act. A shared service, LSB clients are government ministries, the Attorney General, Cabinet, Crown corporations and public agencies. Each law group with the Branch has unique accountabilities and works as a team to support Government with approximately 735 lawyers and support staff.

The Legal Technology Unit has specific responsibility for Legal Services Branch providing expert advice and managing all aspects of the Legal Services Branch's Information Management/Information Technology, including daily business operations, the maintenance and support for line-of-business applications, the enterprise technical architecture and platforms, security administration, change management processes, and technical project management services.

IMIT Generalist Co-op Responsibilities
In an LSB co-op position you will
Work with a dynamic team of information technology professionals and work alongside technical analysts and supporting the project work of Business Consultants and Technical Analysts.
Be involved in development of test plans, testcases and execution of testing.
Be involved in research, analysis, recommendations, and the development of processes/ procedures including process documentation and Standard Operating Procedures (SOP's) for line-of-business applications such as ServiceNow.
Support front end maintenance of websites including updating of CSS and HTML/ASPX.
May be required to do some report generation and basic data analysis.
Be familiar with SQL and SQL server tools.

Preference may be given to candidates applying for their first or second co-op term.

Note 1 This is not a software development posting.
Note 2 Standard workday is 8am-4pm. Some flexibility can be accommodated.

LOCATION Vancouver, BC
TERM Fall Term September - December 2024

At PepsiCo Canada we're always on the lookout for great talent - and that search often brings us to campuses like yours! So, why choose the PepsiCo Supply Chain Engineering Co-op Program?
Early Responsibility Accelerate your career progression with early responsibility and leadership opportunities;
Make an Impact Lead change and shake things up, in a fast paced, dynamic, and evolving industry where you can sink your teeth into challenging opportunities and drive for growth;
Development and Coaching Get coaching from experienced mentors to develop yourself and learn to develop others at a company where our People are a strategic priority;
Mega Brands Make tangible impact on Globally recognized billion-dollar brands! Your friends and family can touch and taste the impact you make!
What will you do in your first experience?
You will be based out of one of our world class manufacturing facilities in Canada. During your term you will gain critical experiences & skills through improving production efficiencies, innovating business solutions, and implementing process changes. In addition, with our Pep+ goals, you can be a part of charting a new course to drive positive action for the plant, our people and our planet. Throughout the term, you will have the opportunity to showcase your tangible impact by presenting on your contributions to the Supply Chain Leadership team!??

This experience will include motivating a frontline team, problem solving issues, and removing barriers in order to help "win the shift".?You will also be responsible for tracking business critical KPIs to identify trends to focus on meaningful projects that advance PepsiCo's journey to be a Faster, Stronger and Better Company.?

Additional Skills & Experiences you will Gain
Building people capability through effective employee relations, training, and communication for front-line hourly personnel
Applying theoretical understanding and practical knowledge of operations, repair procedures, trouble shooting and preventative maintenance to support facility equipment reliability.
Executing Annual Operating Plan for Production
Achieving performance standards with regards to safety, line utilization and quality
Tracking key performance indicators/costs, anticipating and correcting trends which would compromise achievement of financial targets.
Overseeing and assuring facility equipment reliability to minimize downtime.
Ensuring compliance with all Health & Safety regulations
Ensuring compliance with all quality management programs and food safety systems, such as HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points), GMP (Good Manufacturing Practices), and Sanitation Standard Operating Procedures (SSOPs).

j3j8cs.Job Description:
INSPIRE, a program at UVIC, is dedicated to fostering an equitable, diverse, and inclusive science and engineering education. Our Apprentice Garage program offers a unique 4-month work-integrated learning opportunity for students to ideate, design, prototype, and implement solutions that have a profound societal impact. We strongly encourage applications from students from underrepresented groups in STEM, including individuals of marginalized genders, Indigenous peoples, members of racialized minorities, people with disabilities, and LGBTQ2S+ individuals. Participants will receive training in EDI and various development methodologies, including IBM's Enterprise Design Thinking.

Position Overview
We are seeking enthusiastic and dedicated students to join our project about AI for non-profits. In this role, you will collaborate with a diverse group of peers to develop AI solutions tailored for non-profit organizations. This is an excellent opportunity to apply your technical skills to meaningful projects that make a difference in the community.

Key Responsibilities
Team Collaboration Work effectively within a multidisciplinary team, leveraging each member' s strengths to achieve project goals.
AI Development Design, develop, and implement AI models and algorithms to address specific needs of non- profit organizations.
Problem- Solving Engage in problem- solving sessions to identify and analyze challenges faced by partner non- profits, and propose innovative AI- driven solutions.
Client Interaction Communicate with non- profit stakeholders to gather requirements, provide updates, and ensure the AI solutions meet their needs.
Documentation Maintain comprehensive documentation of the development process, including design decisions, code, and user guide.
Non- Profit work Create a generic solution that helps multiple non- profit organizations, ultimately increasing the overall impact of the project.
Testing and Validation Conduct thorough testing and validation of AI models to ensure reliability, accuracy, and ethical compliance.
Training and Support Provide training and support to non- profit partners on the use and maintenance of the developed AI solutions.
j3j8cs.Qualifications:
Qualifications

Currently enrolled in a Computer Science, Software Engineering, Data Science, or related program.
Strong programming skills in languages such as Python, R, or Java is preferred.
Experience with machine learning frameworks and tools ( e. g., Tensor Flow, Py Torch, scikit- learn, Type Script) is an asset but not required.
Excellent teamwork and communication skills.
A passion for using technology for social good.
Prior experience with AI development projects is an asset but not required.
Knowledge of ethical considerations in AI development is preferred.


What We Offer

A collaborative and inclusive work environment that values diversity and innovation.
Hands- on experience with real- world projects that have a positive impact on the community.
Mentorship from industry experts and opportunities for professional growth.
Training in EDI ( Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion) and IBM' s Enterprise Design Thinking methodology.
The chance to make a meaningful difference while developing cutting- edge AI solutions.


Internship Details
This transformative internship offers an unparalleled opportunity to create deep local and potentially global connections, gain hands- on experience, and feel a profound sense of community contribution. As an Apprentice Garage intern, you will commit to this position for 4 months from September to December. The role requires in- person participation. This exclusive role disqualifies you from other co- ops during the term. You may take one additional course in the Fall, provided you can dedicate full- time co- op hours ( 37 hours per week) to the project. The position offers a stipend in the range of $11,500 for the term (details will be provided in the interview).

If you are motivated to contribute to a project that has a direct impact on our community and are ready to challenge yourself in a collaborative, innovative environment, please apply for this significant opportunity. Fill out this application form. Applications will remain open until July 15 , or until the position is filled.

Join us to make a difference and be part of a lasting change in our community!

As the market leader in enterprise application software, we're helping companies of all sizes and in all industries run better by redefining ERP and creating networks of intelligent enterprises that provide transparency, resiliency, and sustainability across supply chains. Project-driven experience gain cross-functional skills from our virtual and in-person learning sessions, diverse subject matter experts, and project deliverables.

The future you want is within reach.

At PCL, we build the places where life happens and find camaraderie in the process. We're a community of builders committed to building better communities. That's why we're always looking ahead, and not just to the next project or what's next in our industry.

We're also looking at what's next for you and how we can help you build a career you're proud of. As a student at PCL, you will get the opportunity for challenging work assignments in a supportive work environment which promotes personal and professional growth.

We are seeking a Estimating Student, for our BC Region district - Kelowna Office, within our Canadian Buildings Division that are available for 4-12 months starting in September 2024. This position will be under the direct supervisor of the Chief Estimator.

Here's how an Estimating Student for our BC Region office contributes to our team

Understanding and utilizing PCL's estimating manuals, systems and forms
Reviewing quantity survey assignment with lead estimator or Chief Estimator
Performing assigned quantity survey
Giving quantities of major purchase items to purchasing department for pricing
Transferring quantities to lead estimator
Performing general subtrade solicitation duties

Performing bid day assignments
Visiting job sites periodically
Developing relationships with subcontractors
Other duties as assigned by the Chief Estimator
Pay Range $20-$26

PCL is an innovative, employee-owned company comprised of people who find passion in their profession and take pride in doing great work every day. Together, we can build success and a better future. Let's get started!

5eedgq.Job Description:
The Company
Demonware is a member of the Activision family of studios. We work alongside engineers and creatives at our AAA partner studios and deliver the online and data services required by our franchises. We craft, develop, and host services for some of the world's biggest video game franchises, including Call of Duty and Crash Bandicoot.

We are a technology company and dedicated to openness. This means collaborating to tackle problems, the freedom to talk to anyone, and the best idea wins. We take the work seriously; ourselves, not so much!

Big company challenges, small company culture
Demonware hosts millions of concurrently connected users. Some of the technologies we use are Python, Linux, MySQL, C++, and Erlang. We solve tough problems on a day-to-day basis, and interns have a huge impact on what we do. We've had interns contribute code and have it running in production within the first three days of joining.

We're open to work terms of 8+ months. If you just graduated, we still want to hear from you!

The Team
The CloudEng team is responsible for all things private and public cloud at Demonware. We architect, build foundations and on-board other engineering teams onto the public cloud, we manage and maintain our on-premise private cloud, and we offer services to enable Demonware developers to build "cloud-ready" game services. We deal with low-level kernel tweaking, we develop self-serve tooling to support massive-scale deployments, and we build standard processes into everything we do.

The Role
Learning to implement foundational infrastructure (DNS, identity/authentication, Artifact management, etc)
Designing and implementing tools and automations running on distributed systems
Finding opportunities to automate time-consuming, manual work
Performing root cause analyses and proposing and/or implementing engineering solutions to prevent or mitigate recurring incidents
Participating in design and code reviews

Perks
Complimentary breakfast, lunch, and drinks from a fully stocked kitchen
BBQs and Lunch & Learns
City-center office location
Vacation allowance
Competitive pay
Free games

We love hearing from anyone who is enthusiastic about changing the games industry. Not sure you meet all qualifications? Let us decide! Research shows that women and members of other under-represented groups tend to not apply to jobs when they think they may not meet every qualification, when, in fact, they often do! At Activision Blizzard, we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive environment and strongly encourage you to apply

Rewards
We provide a suite of benefits that promote physical, emotional and financial well-being for 'Every World' - we've got our employees covered! Subject to eligibility requirements, the Company offers comprehensive benefits including
Medical, dental, vision, health savings account or health reimbursement account, healthcare spending accounts, dependent care spending accounts, life and AD&D insurance, disability insurance;
401(k) with Company match, tuition reimbursement, charitable donation matching;
Paid holidays and vacation, paid sick time, floating holidays, compassion and bereavement leaves, parental leave;
Mental health & wellbeing programs, fitness programs, free and discounted games, and a variety of other voluntary benefit programs like supplemental life & disability, legal service, ID protection, rental insurance, and others;
If the Company requires that you move geographic locations for the job, then you may also be eligible for relocation assistance.
Eligibility to participate in these benefits may vary for part time and temporary full-time employees and interns with the Company. You can learn more by visiting https//www.benefitsforeveryworld.com/.

Together, we can build success and a better future. Let's get started!

3n0zpl.Job Description:
Co-op Video Codec Engineer

This is a 8-16 months full-time Co-op employment opportunity starting September 2024.

This position is open for both Burnaby, BC and Markham, ON offices.

About NETINT Technologies Inc.
NETINT Technologies is a developer of intelligent silicon solutions for data-intensive applications. Its Codensity portfolio enables enterprise, cloud data centers, and content providers to deploy high-performance applications that they can scale without limit while minimizing their data storage and processing costs. NETINT, founded by an experienced team of storage SoC veterans, is a Canadian venture-funded startup with R&D facilities in Vancouver and Toronto.

NETINT offers competitive salaries, benefits, and endless learning and development opportunities.

Job Scope
As a Co-op Video Codec Engineer, you will be part of a team in video codec design and optimization in NETINT Technologies. You will work closely with architects, algorithm engineers, and other codec engineers in video/image compression and optimization.

Responsibilities
Participate in video/image codec design and write design documents
Use Intel assembly instruction set to speedup software encoders
Develop video/image codec algorithm and optimization.
Debug software and conduct software unit test
Write test cases for design verification and compliance tests
Write Python test scripts to automate tests
3n0zpl.Qualifications:
Qualifications
MS or BS in Electrical Engineering or Computer Engineering/Science
Good knowledge on image/video compression and some knowledge on hardware
Experience on assembly language programming
Proficient in software programming (C/C++/Python)
Hands-on experience in debugging and scripting
Good oral and written skills for communication and documentation

Desirable Skills/Experiences
Experience in signal processing, multimedia, or video/image compression
Experience in video/image quality evaluation

We thank you for your interest, however, only candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Acknowledgment and Agreement
NETINT Technologies Inc. is an equal opportunity employer and strives to ensure that its hiring process meets the needs of all persons with disabilities. As such, NETINT will provide reasonable accommodation for any applicant, as requested during the hiring process.

About this Position
This is an 8 to 16 months full-time, Co-op employment opportunity starting September 2024.

This position is open for both Burnaby, BC and Markham, ON offices.

About NETINT Technologies Inc.
NETINT Technologies is a developer of intelligent silicon solutions for data-intensive applications. Its Codensity portfolio enables enterprise, cloud data centers, and content providers to deploy high-performance applications that they can scale without limit while minimizing their data storage and processing costs. NETINT, founded by an experienced team of storage SoC veterans, is a Canadian venture-funded startup with R&D facilities in Vancouver and Toronto.

NETINT offers competitive salaries, benefits, and endless learning and development opportunities.

Job Scope
As a Co-op Firmware Engineer, you will be part of a team developing high performance embedded software running on SoCs designed by NETINT Technologies. You will work closely with architects, ASIC design engineers, and other firmware engineers in implementing and verifying the firmware design.

Responsibilities
Participate in firmware design and write design documents
Develop firmware that runs on a simulated environment, FPGA and SoC.
Debug firmware
Conduct firmware unit test
Write test cases for design verification and compliance tests
Write Python test scripts to automate tests

Experience with Agile software development methods
